title: The KQL Pipe Model
summary: A KQL tabular expression usually starts with a table and then pipes rows through operators using |.
analogy: It is an assembly line. Raw rows enter on the left; each station filters, labels, groups, or packages them; the final result exits on the right.
diagram:
flowchart LR
  Table[Table] --> Where[where filter rows]
  Where --> Extend[extend add columns]
  Extend --> Summarize[summarize group rows]
  Summarize --> Project[project choose columns]
  Project --> Take[take limit rows]
body:
## First principles

Analytical questions are transformations: start with rows, keep some, add facts, group them, shape the answer, and limit output. KQL makes each transformation explicit and ordered.

## Definition

A KQL tabular expression usually starts with a table and then pipes rows through operators using |. Common flow is Table | where filter | extend computed columns | summarize aggregates | project output columns | top or take a safe number of rows.

## How it fits together

Read KQL left to right. Every operator receives a table and returns a table, so the next operator has fewer, richer, or differently shaped rows.

## Worked example

```kql
Requests
| where Timestamp > ago(2h)
| extend Slow = DurationMs > 1000
| summarize Total = count(), SlowRequests = countif(Slow) by bin(Timestamp, 10m), OperationName
| project Timestamp, OperationName, Total, SlowRequests
| top 20 by SlowRequests desc
```

*A practical pipe for recent high-latency requests.*

drills.q: What does the pipe character do?
drills.a: It sends the table result from one operator into the next operator.
Filtering early reduces rows for later operators and often improves performance.
Aggregated rows, often grouped by one or more columns.

teachItBack: KQL is a left-to-right table pipeline. Each pipe step transforms rows into a new table. I should filter early, aggregate deliberately, and limit final output.

glossary.definition: A KQL expression whose input and output are tables.
A KQL step such as where, project, summarize, or join.
The | symbol that passes tabular output to the next operator.

quiz.q: Which Azure RBAC role lets the identity read cluster resource metadata without changing resources?
quiz.explanation: Reader includes control-plane read actions and no write permissions.
